steps.text: Clear a square of floor in {room} big enough for you and {friend}.
Gather four pillows and two blankets and stack them in your spot.
Stand the pillows on edge to make four fort walls.
Drape one blanket across the top for the roof.
What kind of fort is this for {friend}?
Tuck a book and a flashlight inside. Crawl in with {friend} and read one page out loud.
Cut a peephole between two pillows. Take turns spying out with {friend} and call out three things you see.
Gather two sheets of paper and one crayon. Sit at a flat surface with {friend}.
Fold the first sheet in half the long way, then unfold it.
Fold both top corners down to meet the center crease.
Fold the slanted edges down again to make a sharp nose.
What kind of plane is this?
Fold the wings flat and crisp the creases with your thumbnail.
Bend the back wing tips up a little so the plane wants to climb.
Color a number on each wing — that is your plane's flight number.
Hold the plane behind your ear, point at {friend}, and let it fly. Cheer when it lands!
{friend} wants to play a color hunt. Pick a color to chase first.
Walk slowly around {room} looking for red. Touch each red thing and count out loud.
Walk slowly around {room} looking for blue. Touch each blue thing and count out loud.
Walk slowly around {room} looking for yellow. Touch each yellow thing and count out loud.
Bring {count} of your favorites back and lay them in a line in front of {friend}.
Tell {friend} which one was the easiest to find and which was the trickiest.
Find a tray, plate, or shoebox lid to be your garden frame.
Collect six small pebbles, beads, or buttons and pour them in.
Add one leaf, one twig, or one folded paper triangle as a tree.
Push the pebbles around to make a path winding through the middle.
What lives in this garden with {friend}?
Pile three pebbles into a dragon hill at the path's end. Place {friend} as the dragon's friend.
Make four small pebble nests off the path. Tuck {friend} in as the kindly grandmouse.
Gather {count} small objects from {room} into a messy pile near {friend}.
How should we sort the pile?
Line everything up smallest on the left, biggest on the right.
Line everything up palest on the left, darkest on the right.
Line everything up softest on the left, hardest on the right.
Walk {friend} along the line and announce each thing in order.
Lay paper and three crayons in front of you. {friend} is the art director.
Draw a big round body in the middle of the page.
Add {count} eyes anywhere on the body.
Give the monster four legs — straight, wiggly, or zig-zag, your call.
What does your monster eat?
Draw three purple snacks floating near the monster's mouth.
Draw three perfectly round snacks floating near the monster's mouth.
Whisper the monster's name to {friend}, then say it loud and proud.
Stand in {room} with {friend} and look at the open floor.
What kind of course should we build?
Set two pillows as crawl-under tunnels and one rolled blanket as a jump bar.
Lay a long string or belt on the floor as a wobbly tightrope and place a book at each end.
Walk the course slowly first to remember every station.
Run the course as fast as you can while {friend} cheers from the sidelines.
Take a bow. Tell {friend} the hardest part and the easiest part.
Find a cup, a bowl, or a jar to be your cauldron.
Add three pretend ingredients from {room} — a sock, a paper scrap, a crayon stub.
Stir slowly with a spoon or stick while you whisper the recipe to {guide_mentor}.
What does this potion do?
Pretend to drink it, then run a victory lap around {room}.
Pretend to drink it, then crouch behind a {prop} and giggle while {guide_mentor} 'looks' for you.
Pretend to pour it on a houseplant, then slowly stand tall with arms out like branches.
Sit on the floor with paper, a crayon, and {friend} the cartographer.
Draw the outline of {room} as a big rectangle on the page.
Add the door, the window, and one piece of furniture as boxes inside.
Pick a hiding spot in {room} and hide a small {prop} there.
Draw a big X on the map at the hiding spot.
How does the treasure hunter find the X?
Draw {count} numbered footprints from the door to the X. Hand the map to a grown-up to follow.
Write one clue word at the top of the map. Hand the map to a grown-up and watch them puzzle it out.
Set up a pretend stovetop on a low table or chair seat with {friend} as the customer.
Gather four pretend ingredients — pebbles, paper balls, leaves, anything.
What is on the menu today?
Drop the ingredients into a pretend pot. Stir slowly and hum a cooking tune.
Press the ingredients flat onto a pretend pan. Flip each one with a big wrist toss.
Slide your dish onto a small plate or paper square in front of {friend}.
Take {friend}'s order review — five stars or four? Bow to the chef!
Find a tall cardboard box, paper-towel tube, or stacked cushions to be your rocket body.
Tape or balance a paper triangle on top as the nose cone.
Add three paper rectangles around the base as fins.
Strap {sidekick} into the rocket as the brave astronaut.
Count down from ten with one finger raised per number.
Where does the rocket fly today?
Bounce around {room} on tip-toe like low gravity. Plant a paper flag wherever you land.
Crouch low, then stretch up tall like blooming. Tell {sidekick} which giant flower is friendliest.
Collect paper-towel tubes, books, and a small ball or marble.
Lean two books to make a ramp from a chair down to the floor.
Lay one tube along the ramp so the marble can roll through it.
Where should the marble land at the bottom?
Tape a paper cup at the end of the tube. Aim carefully and roll the marble in.
Place a pillow under the tube's end. Watch the marble bounce-soft and stop.
Drop the marble three times in a row and count how many times it lands cleanly.
Tell {friend} the number out loud and high-five for the best run.
Find a small flat surface — a tray, a windowsill, a low shelf — for the display.
Walk through {room} (or near a window) and gather {count} natural-feeling things: leaves, pebbles, pinecones, feathers.
Lay everything out on the surface in any order.
Pick your favorite find and place it in the very center.
How should we share the display with {friend}?

choices.label: Make a tiny label for each

steps.text: Tear small paper squares and write or draw a label per item. Place each label beside its find.
Pick up each item one at a time and tell {friend} a one-sentence story about where it came from.
Tear paper into four small cards. Sit on the floor with {friend} and a crayon.
Draw a different dance move on each card — a spin, a hop, a wiggle, a freeze.
What kind of music plays while you dance?
Hum or sing fast bouncy tune. Flip a card and do the move.
Hum a slow floaty tune. Flip a card and do the move slooowly.
Clap a steady beat. Flip a card and match the move to your claps.
Flip the rest of the cards one by one until you have danced every move.
Bow to {friend} and stack the cards into a tidy deck for next time.
Bring four picture books and place them open in a half-circle in {room}.
Set one stuffed animal or {friend} inside each open book — those are the habitats.
Walk in a slow loop around the zoo and announce each habitat by name.
Pick the animal that looks hungriest and pretend to feed them three pretend snacks.
What special zoo event happens next?
Carry {friend} on your shoulder and march past every habitat clapping and cheering.
Tip-toe past every habitat whispering goodnight. Tuck {friend} on top of the last book.
Collect six paper cups, plastic cups, or yogurt pots and rinse-clean them if needed.
Set the cups upside-down in a {room} corner. Those are the buildings.
What kind of city is this?
Cluster the cups close together in a tight grid. Add tiny paper-square doors.
Line the cups in two long rows with a 'beach' (a folded blue cloth) between.
Stack cups in a pyramid. Tape paper antennas on top of the highest one.
Lay strips of paper or string between buildings as roads or paths.
Walk {friend} along the streets like a tour guide and name three buildings out loud.
Find a sunny spot near a window in {room} with paper and a crayon.
Stand a small object — a cup, a {prop}, a toy — on the paper so its shadow falls on the page.
Hold the object steady and trace its shadow with the crayon.
Move the object and try a different angle, then trace again.
What should the shadow drawing become?
Add eyes, fangs, and ears inside the shadow outline. Show {friend} the new monster.
Write one tiny letter inside each shadow outline. Hand the page to a grown-up to decode.
What kind of pretend snack are we making with {friend}?
Pretend to drop three colors of fruit into a blender cup. Make whirring sounds with your mouth.
Pretend to stack three layers — bread, jelly, more bread — patting each layer flat.
Give the snack a silly name. Say it three times so {friend} can remember.
Draw the snack on a paper card and write the silly name underneath.
Hand the recipe card to {friend} for the cookbook.
Gather all the stuffed animals you can carry into one pile in {room}.
Sit cross-legged with {friend} on top of the pile as the leader.
How should the animals be stacked?
Stack one animal at a time as high as you dare. Hold your breath at the wobbly part.
Set three on the bottom, two in the middle, one on top. Step back and check the shape.
Pile every animal into a snuggly heap. Wiggle in the middle yourself.
Touch each animal and say its name out loud — invent one for any stranger.
Find a balloon (or a rolled-up sock) for the indoor ball.
Mark two goals — one on each side of {room} — using shoes or pillows.
{friend} drops the ball at center floor. Crouch ready on opposite sides.
What is the rule for moving the ball?
Kick gently — the first goal scored ends the round.
Bat softly — the first goal scored ends the round.
Crawl and blow — the first goal scored ends the round.
Fold a piece of paper in half. Sit beside {friend} the design assistant.
Draw half a person along the fold — head, body, one arm, one leg.
Cut along the outline (with help if scissors are tricky), then unfold.
Color the doll's face with eyes, nose, and a smile.
What is this paper friend wearing today?
Tear a small triangle of paper, color it, and tape it to the doll's shoulders.
Draw flowers and ruffles down the doll's body. Pick a name and tell {friend}.
Stand face to face with {friend} (or a grown-up) in the middle of {room}.
Pick a starting move — a regular high-five works great.
Add a second move: maybe a low-five or a fist bump.
Add a third move: a spin, a jump, a wiggle — anything!
Pick a big finishing move for the handshake.
Run the whole handshake start to end three times. End each time with the loud clap-and-shout.
Run the whole handshake start to end three times. End each time with the slow bow.
Pile every pillow you can find into one big mountain in {room}.
Plant a paper flag (a folded sheet on a pencil) at the very top.
Walk slowly around the mountain three times to scout the climb.
Pick a 'base camp' on one side and place {friend} there.
Climb (or scoot) carefully from base camp toward the flag.
What do you do when you reach the summit?

choices.label: Slide back down on your bottom

steps.text: Stand tall, beat your chest, and let out one giant ROAR. {friend} cheers from base camp.
Sit down on the top pillow and slide-tumble back to {friend}, giggling the whole way.
Find an empty cup, jar, or bowl to be your collecting jar.
Tear paper into ten small pieces and crumple them into pretend leaves.
Drop the pretend leaves into the jar with {friend} as the field assistant.
Cut three tiny paper bugs — a beetle, a worm, a ladybug — and tuck them in.
How will you cover the bug jar?
Tear a square of paper towel and rest it gently on top. Show {friend} how the bugs can breathe.
Lay a square of plastic wrap (or tissue paper) on top. Watch the bugs through the window with {friend}.
Roll three pairs of clean socks into three soft balls in {room}.
How should the relay course look?
Line the sock-balls up at the start. Carry one at a time to {friend} as fast as you can.
Set three pillows in a zig-zag. Weave each sock-ball through to {friend}.
Set up a chair tunnel. Push each sock-ball under it on your hands and knees.
Count the sock-balls now sitting with {friend}. Cheer your finishing time.
Pick a shoebox, a drawer, or an open book as the dollhouse frame.
What kind of room is this?
Fold a tissue into a tiny bed. Add a pencil-eraser pillow.
Stand a bottle cap on its side as a stove. Tear paper triangles for pizza slices.
Roll a paper square into a tiny canvas. Set crayon-stub paint pots beside it.
Move {friend} (or a small toy) into the room as the resident.
Give a tour out loud — three things {friend} can see from inside.
Find a clear cup or jar and a piece of cotton (or torn tissue) for clouds.
Tear blue paper into tiny rain-drop scraps and a yellow paper sun.
Layer the cotton 'cloud' near the top, sun on one side, rain scraps below.
What kind of weather is brewing in this jar?
Push the sun to the front and the cloud to the side. Tell {friend} the picnic plan.
Push the cloud to the front. Tap the jar like raindrops while {friend} hides under a pillow umbrella.

templates.title: Set up a tiny lending library

steps.text: Stack five small books on a low shelf or floor patch with {helper_townsperson}.
Make a paper sign that says LIBRARY and tape it above the stack.
Tear five paper squares as borrower cards — one per book.
What special library event happens today?
Pick one book, sit {helper_townsperson} beside you, and read or tell the first page out loud.
Line the books up cover-out. Have {helper_townsperson} pick the best cover and award it a paper ribbon.
Stand in a clear corner of {room} with {friend} as the photographer.
Pretend to hold up a camera and say 'CLICK' to start a pose.
What is the photo theme today?
Hold three different superhero poses. Freeze for three seconds each.
Pose as three different animals. Freeze and make the animal sound.
Make three of the silliest faces you can. Freeze long enough for {friend} to 'click'.
Pretend to scroll through the photos with {friend} and pick the best one.
Sit at a flat surface with paper, {friend}, and a calm minute.
Fold a paper square diagonally to make a triangle.
Fold the two side corners up to meet at the top — that's a paper boat-ish shape.
Open the bottom flap and pinch into a small cup.
What goes in your origami cup?
Hunt for three pocket-size treasures and drop them in the cup.
Write or draw a tiny message on a paper scrap and fold it into the cup.
Hold the cup with two hands and present it to {friend} like a royal gift.
Find {count} small flat-ish things — books, coasters, paper plates — and a stretch of clear floor.
Stand still in {room} with {friend} watching from a safe spot.
Practice balancing the first item on your {body_part} for a count of three.
Now what? Stack more, or take a walk?

choices.label: Walk one step at a time

steps.text: Add the items one by one to your {body_part}. Hold steady until {friend} counts to {count}.
Take {count} slow steps with one item on your {body_part}. {friend} cheers each step.
Stand in {room} with {friend} as the chain leader.
Pick a starting spot and {action_verb} once in place.
What pattern does the chain follow?
{action_verb} {count} times in a straight line. Land each one a step further from where you started.
{action_verb} {count} times in a circle around {friend}. Each one in a new spot on the loop.
Bow to {friend} and shout the number out loud — {count}!
Pick a starting {prop} and place it on the floor in one corner of {room}.
Find a second prop-like object and lay it beside the first, just touching.
Keep adding objects in a long line — anything small and safe — for {count} pieces.
Walk slowly along the line beside {friend} and name each object out loud.
What is the chain for?
Carry {friend} along the chain in tiny hops, stopping once at every object.
Make up a one-sentence chapter for each object as you walk past. End with the last object as the big twist.
Pick {count} flat spots in {room} — corners, chairs, books on the floor.
At each spot, place one object that feels {adjective}.
Walk {friend} from station to station and touch each object once.
What do we do at the stations?
Spin one full turn at each station with {friend} held high.
Lean in and whisper one secret to {friend} at each station.
Strike a different pose at each station and freeze for two seconds.
Pick a paper, a tube, or a sock — your craft needs to help you {action_verb}.
How will the craft work?
Roll the paper into a hand-held tool. Tape it shut and decorate the grip.
Cut and tape a paper band that fits on your {body_part}. Add a little flair.
Use your new craft to {action_verb} three times in front of {friend}.
Bow. Tell {friend} what to call this invention.
Lay paper down in {room}. {guide_mentor} is the curator.
Press your {body_part} gently on a damp paper towel, then onto the dry paper to leave a print.
Make {count} prints in a row across the page.
How do we label the museum prints?
Write a number under each print. Walk {guide_mentor} along the gallery and read each number aloud.
Invent a silly monster name for each print and write or whisper it to {guide_mentor}.
Gather {count} prop-style objects in a pile in {room}.
Sit {quest_giver} on a chair as the royal audience.
What kind of parade is this?
Hold each prop high and march past {quest_giver} stamping your feet. Cycle through all {count}.
Hold each prop with two hands and glide past {quest_giver} on tip-toe. Cycle through all {count}.
Stack the props in front of {quest_giver} and give a royal salute.
Tear a sheet of paper into {count} smaller squares. Sit beside {friend}.
On the first square, draw something that looks {adjective}.
On the second, draw something else that looks {adjective} — but in a different way.
Keep going until all {count} squares are full of {adjective} pictures.
How do we display the pictures?

choices.label: Tape them in a long row

steps.text: Tape the squares in a long row on a wall or floor. Walk {friend} along like a gallery tour.
Fan the squares around {friend} in a half-circle. Spin slowly and call out which one is the {adjective}-est.
Sit very still in the middle of {room} with {friend} and a pencil.
Close your eyes for ten quiet seconds and listen.
Open your eyes. Tell {friend} {count} sounds you heard.
Draw a quick map of {room} with a tiny picture for each sound's source.
What should the sound map become?
Cross out the loudest sound. Whisper a goodnight to each remaining one with {friend}.
Tip-toe to each sound's source and tap it once. {friend} keeps score on the map.
Pick a small soft prop — a sock, a paper ball, a small pillow.
Set the prop on the floor in {room}. {friend} watches from the side.
How tricky should this be?
Use only your {body_part} to roll the prop across {room} to {friend}.
Use only your {body_part} to lift the prop onto a chair near {friend}. No hands cheating!
Balance the prop on your {body_part}, then move three steps without dropping it.
Pick one tidy mission for {room}.

choices.label: Three soft things to a basket

steps.text: Find {count} soft things and carry them to a basket. {helper_townsperson} cheers each one home.
Find {count} stray books and slot them onto a shelf. {helper_townsperson} reads the title aloud each time.
Sit knee-to-knee with {friend} in {room}.
How do we earn a smile?
Tell {friend} the silliest one-line joke you know. Wait for the giggle.
Wiggle your whole body for ten counts. {friend}'s smile is your prize.
Stand in the doorway of {room} with {friend} on alert.
Look around and call out the first round thing you spot.
What now?
Pick up {count} round things and pile them in front of {friend}. Take a bow.
Point at each round thing and count out loud until you reach {count}.
Empty a sock drawer (or a sock pile) onto the floor of {room}.
How do we sort the socks?
Match each sock to its twin. Roll each pair into a ball and stack them up. {helper_townsperson} keeps the count.
Line the socks up in rainbow order — red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple. Show {helper_townsperson} the streak.
Take one sheet of paper. Sit beside {friend}.
Fold the paper in half across the long way, then fold the top corners down to the center.
How does the hat finish?

choices.label: Wear it as a sailor hat
Flip it for a wizard hat

steps.text: Fold the bottom flaps up. Pop the hat open and put it on. Salute {friend}.
Stand the hat tall like a cone and balance it on your head. Wave a pretend wand at {friend}.
Clear a big rectangle of floor in {room}. {quest_giver} is the royal architect.
Carry over four cushions for the foundation walls.
Place the cushions in a hollow square. Step inside to test the size.
Add a row of books along the top of two cushions to make taller walls.
Drape a blanket across two walls as the throne-room roof.
Place a small stool or pile of pillows inside as the throne for {quest_giver}.
What kind of tower does the castle need?
Stack three more pillows or boxes by one wall. The taller the better.
Lay two cushions side by side across the entrance with a gap to crawl through.
Tuck a blanket-covered chair behind the back wall. Mark the entrance with a {prop}.
Cut paper triangles and tape them to walls as banners. Pick royal colors.
Crown {quest_giver} on the throne with a paper-strip crown and bow three times.

templates.title: Set up a make-believe market stall

steps.text: Pick a small table or low chair-back as your market counter.
Tear paper into ten small price tags and set them in a stack.
Gather six pretend goods — fruit toys, blocks, paper-ball muffins, anything.
Arrange the goods in a tidy row across the counter.
Write or draw a price on each tag and place it in front of its goods.
Tape a paper sign above the counter with the stall's name.
What is the stall's specialty today?
Set a fluffy pillow loaf in the center. Pretend to slice it for any customer.
Cut paper diamonds and color them shiny. Lay them on a folded napkin tray.
{helper_townsperson} comes to shop. Greet them, show three options, and wrap their pick in paper.
Hand {helper_townsperson} the package and wave goodbye. Count the pretend coins they 'paid'.
Find a sink, a bathtub, or a wide bowl of water. Bring two sheets of paper and {friend}.
Fold the first sheet in half the long way, then unfold.
Fold the top corners down to the center crease — that's the boat's nose.
Fold the bottom strips up on each side to make the boat's rim.
Pull the rim apart and pinch the bottom flat — your first boat is ready.
Repeat the folds with the second sheet to make boat number two.
Color the sails or hulls so the boats look different.
What kind of race is this?
Set both boats at one end. Blow gently and cheer the first boat to touch the far wall.
Stir the water gently in a circle. Count how many laps each boat makes before stopping.
Lift the winning boat out and present it to {friend}. Drip-dry it on a paper towel.
Set out a brush, a small towel, and a paper-bow ribbon in {room}. {friend} is first in line.
Greet {friend} at the salon door and lead them to the towel mat.
Brush {friend}'s fur (or fabric) gently in long strokes. Hum a salon song.
Pretend to wash {friend}'s ears with a small dab of pretend shampoo.
Pat {friend} dry with the towel. Spin them gently to check the look.
What style does {friend} want today?
Tease the fur outward with the brush. Tie the paper bow in a big poof on top.
Press the fur smooth with your hand. Tie the paper bow flat at the side.
Brush in zig-zag swoops. Tie the paper bow in a crooked, fun knot.
Hold {friend} up to a mirror (or pretend mirror) for the big reveal.
Wave {friend} out the door and call the next pretend pet for an appointment.
Tape three sheets of paper edge-to-edge along a wall or floor strip in {room}.
Sit at one end with {friend} and a stack of crayons or markers.
Draw a long horizontal line across all three sheets — that is the ground.
On the first sheet, draw two small houses with square windows.
On the second sheet, draw a park with three trees and a bench.
On the third sheet, draw a tall building or two — the downtown skyline.
Add roads connecting the buildings along the ground line.
What's the weather in your paper town?
Draw a big sun in the corner and a couple of fluffy clouds across the sky.
Draw rain lines from the clouds and tiny puddles along the road.
Cover the ground with little dots of snow and add a snowman beside the park.
Draw three stick-figure people doing different things on the road.
Walk {friend} from one end of the mural to the other. Tell the story of each piece.
Find {count} leaves of different shapes in the yard or near a window plant.
Lay each leaf flat on a paper bag. Press a crayon sideways across the paper to print the leaf shape.
Ask {guide_mentor} which leaf print looks the most {adjective}.
What kind of collage will it become?
Add stick lines between leaves to make tree trunks. Show {guide_mentor} the woods you grew.
Arrange the leaves from biggest to smallest in a curve. Tell {guide_mentor} the rainbow's name.
Find a small cup of buttons or beads. If none, use bits of paper torn into circles.
Draw a {adjective} shape on a paper plate with {friend}.
Fill the shape inside its outline by laying buttons one at a time.
Count out loud as you place every {count}th button.
Step back and admire it with {friend}.
Ask {guide_mentor} to drop an ice cube on a small plate.
Hold one finger one inch above the ice. Don't touch — just feel how cold the air is.
What test should we try?

choices.label: Press a warm spoon on top

steps.text: Sprinkle a pinch of salt on the cube. Watch the surface bubble. Tell {guide_mentor} what changed.
Press the back of a warm spoon on the ice. Notice the dent. Show {guide_mentor} the shape you made.
Find a sunny spot near a window with {guide_mentor}.
Stand a {prop} on the floor so it makes a tall shadow.
Trace the shadow with a crayon on a paper underneath.
Wait ten slow breaths together. Then trace the shadow again.
Show {guide_mentor} the two shapes. Ask why they aren't the same.
Borrow two clean socks from the laundry. Slide one over each hand.
Give your puppets {adjective} names. Tell {friend} which is which.
Make the puppets shake hands. Bow to each other.
What story should they tell?

choices.label: A meeting on a tall hill

steps.text: Have one puppet climb your other arm like a hill. The other waves from the top to {friend}.
Have the puppets search behind a pillow. One finds an invisible umbrella. {friend} gets to name it.
Ask {guide_mentor} for {count} paperclips.
Link them end to end into one long chain.
Lay the chain along the edge of a book. How many clips long is the book?
Now measure your own foot. Tell {guide_mentor} the number.
Find one more object the same length as your foot.
Find two cardboard tubes. If only one, you and {friend} can each hold half.
Tape the tubes side by side so they look like binoculars.
Hold them up to your eyes. Walk slowly around the room.
What kind of spy are you?
Spot {count} things shaped like a bird with {friend}. A pillow can count if it's lumpy enough.
Find {count} hidden treasures with {friend}. Whisper to each other what they are.
Ask {helper_townsperson} for a small handful of dry pasta in two shapes.
Lay them out on a plate. Sort each shape into its own pile.
Make a pattern: one of shape A, one of shape B, repeating in a line.
Read the pattern out loud as you point to each piece.
Mix the piles again so {helper_townsperson} can try next.
Stand near a window with {friend}.
Find one red thing. Point to it.
Find one orange thing. Yellow next.
Now green, blue, and purple. Help {friend} if a color is missing — pretend something is that color.
Stand together and name the six colors out loud in order.
Get one paper napkin and place it flat on the table.
Fold it in half the long way.
Roll one end inward like a small log.
Stand it up. Does it stay? Try fixing it with {guide_mentor}.
Place the standing napkin at a chair as a fancy table setting.
Trace your hand and forearm onto a paper to make a tree trunk and branches.
Trace {friend}'s hand next to yours so two trunks grow together.
Add scribbled green dots at the branch tips for leaves.
Give the tree a {adjective} name with {friend}.
Hang it on a wall or fridge to remember.
Fold a small square of paper into a cube shape with {guide_mentor}'s help.
Write one to six dots on each side.
Roll the dice on the floor.
What does the number mean?

choices.label: That many breaths in slow motion

steps.text: Do that many hops in a row. {guide_mentor} can count out loud.
Take that many slow breaths. Notice how your tummy moves. Tell {guide_mentor}.
Gather {count} cushions or pillows.
Stack them tallest on the bottom, smallest on top.
How tall is the stack? Stand next to it with {friend}.
Take the top one off. Then the next. Knock the rest down all at once.
Rebuild it once more with {friend} cheering.
Ask {guide_mentor} for one small cup of water and a drinking straw.
Put one end of the straw in the cup.
Blow gently into the top of the straw. Watch the water bubble.
Stop and tell {guide_mentor} what made the bubbles.
Try one more time, but blow softer. Compare the two.
Take a black or dark paper. Press white stickers or chalk dots all over it.
Draw lines between dots to make {count} pictures in the stars with {guide_mentor}.
Give each constellation a {adjective} name.
Hold the paper up high. Pretend you're looking at the sky.
Show {guide_mentor} your favorite constellation and explain why.
Find a small jar or cup. Place a wet napkin inside.
Tuck one bean or seed between the napkin and the side of the cup.
Place it where {guide_mentor} can find a sunny spot.
Draw a tiny picture of what the seed looks like today.
Promise to check it tomorrow and the next day with {guide_mentor}.
Find a basket of clean socks with {helper_townsperson}.
Sort them into piles by color.
Count how many socks are in the biggest pile.
Find matching pairs in each pile.
Give the unmatched socks to {helper_townsperson} as a pretend mystery.
Cut {count} small rectangles from a piece of paper.
Draw a {adjective} face on each one. Write a number in the corner.
Stack the bills neatly with {friend}.
What will the money buy?
{friend} is the shopkeeper. Trade one bill for a pretend cone.
{friend} hands you a real book in exchange. Read one page out loud.
Get a strip of paper. Curl your fingers and dip them in pretend paint.
Press fingertips down to make {count} tiny tracks across the paper.
What kind of animal made these tracks? Tell {guide_mentor}.
Make a different track with your knuckle. That's a big animal.
Make a wavy track with your finger pad. That's a slithering one.
Sit cross-legged on the floor near {guide_mentor}.
Tap two fingertips on your knees in a slow beat: tap-tap-tap.
Hum any quiet note along with the taps.
Slow it down even more. Two breaths between taps.
Fold a small piece of paper into a tiny bird shape with {friend}.
Pinch the wings to lift them slightly.
Make {count} birds total. Sit them in a row on a windowsill.
Where are they flying?
Slide the birds toward a sunny patch with {friend}. Say goodbye until spring.
Stack a few books for a mountain. Place birds on the highest book with {friend}.
Spread {count} pillows around the floor with {friend}.
Pick a start spot and a finish spot.
Draw a quick map showing your path between the pillows.
Follow the map carefully — step only where you drew steps.
Switch maps with {friend}. Run each other's courses.
Find {count} small slips of paper. Each is a letter.
Draw a {adjective} picture on each one.
Fold them in half. Hand them to {helper_townsperson} the postal worker.
{helper_townsperson} carries the letters to a 'mailbox' across the room.
Open each letter and read the picture story out loud.
Make a circle from a {prop} on the floor. That's your spaceship.
Sit in the middle with {friend}. Buckle pretend seatbelts.
Count down from {count}. Lift off!
Which planet first?
Lean over the edge with {friend} and dip a finger in the pretend sea.
Climb out and take giant slow hops with {friend} like there's no gravity.
Get a paper. Write 'Recipe for {adjective} Soup' across the top.
List {count} pretend ingredients with {helper_townsperson}.
Add silly steps: stir three times, hop once, whisper a secret to the pot.
Read the whole recipe out loud to {helper_townsperson}.
Pretend to taste it. Add one more pinch of something silly.
Hide {count} small {prop}s around the room with {quest_giver}'s help.
Get a small spoon — that's the digging tool.
Pretend the floor is hot sand and the {prop}s are fossils.
Dig up each one. Brush it gently with your fingers.
Line up the fossils. Tell {quest_giver} which dinosaur each came from.
Lay a long {prop} on the floor in a straight line.
Walk heel-to-toe along the line with arms out.
Now walk it backward slowly. {friend} watches and counts.
Try one more time with eyes half closed. Wobble allowed.
Trade places — {friend} walks while you count.
Find a small shoebox. Open it on the floor.
Place {count} tiny objects inside that might tell a story.
Pick one object. That's the hero. The next is the friend.
Tell {guide_mentor} the start of a story using only the objects.
Close the box. Whisper the ending into it.

templates.title: Paper boat regatta in the sink

steps.text: Ask {guide_mentor} to fill a bowl or sink with a thumb of water.
Fold a small piece of paper into a boat shape.
Make a second boat. Decorate with a {adjective} stripe.
Float both boats. Blow gently to race them.
Tell {guide_mentor} which boat won and which was prettier.
Stand facing {friend}.
Pick an animal in your head. Don't say which.
Move like that animal. No sounds, just motion.
Give {friend} {count} guesses.
Switch turns. Now {friend} acts and you guess.
Find a shallow plate. That's the garden plot.
Stand {count} pencils upright in the plate as trees.
Tear paper into small green squares. Sprinkle them as grass.
Add one button or pebble as a {adjective} stone.
Walk a finger through the garden. Tell {helper_townsperson} what you see there.
Find {count} index cards or thin paper rectangles.
Lean two cards against each other to make a tent shape.
Make a second tent next to the first.
How tall to build?
Carefully balance two cards across the tents. Show {friend} the second floor.
Stand back and count the rooms with {friend}.
Sit by a window with {guide_mentor}.
Look outside without talking for ten slow breaths.
Whisper one thing you notice moving.
Whisper one thing you notice that is very still.
Take a big paper. Fold it diagonally with {friend}.
Tuck the bottom edges up to form a triangle hat.
Decorate with crayon stripes or stars.
Make a second hat for {friend} the same way.
Put them on. March in a tiny parade across the room.
Sit at a table with paper and a crayon. {guide_mentor} hums any tune.
Move the crayon in time with the humming. Fast or slow, your call.
When the humming stops, lift the crayon.
Look at the lines you drew with {guide_mentor}.
Hum together while pointing along the lines from start to end.
{quest_giver} hands you a folded paper with a {adjective} clue.
Read the clue and look around. The clue points to a thing in the room.
Find that thing. Under it is another clue from {quest_giver}.
What kind of treasure is it?
Lift up the last clue. Underneath, pretend a tiny gem is glowing. Show {quest_giver}.
The last clue is a tiny note. Read it out loud to {quest_giver}.
Drape a blanket over two chairs to make a tent with {friend}.
Crawl inside with a flashlight or a phone flashlight.
Stack a few crumpled paper balls — that's the campfire.
Tell {friend} one tiny story while looking at the 'fire'.
Pretend to roast a marshmallow. Hand one to {friend}.
Write {count} simple moves on small slips: hop, spin, clap, stomp.
Shuffle the slips and lay them in a row.
Do the moves in order with {friend}.
Shuffle the slips again. Do the new order, faster.
Take a bow with {friend}.
Set out a small plate and a folded napkin.
Draw a menu of {count} pretend dishes with {helper_townsperson}.
{helper_townsperson} is the customer. Greet them politely.
Take their order. Pretend to write it down.
Serve the plate with a fancy 'enjoy!' and bow.
Fold one paper into your usual airplane shape.
Fold a second one with one extra fold for stubby wings.
Throw both from the same spot. Which flies farther?
What changes the flight?
Add a tiny paperclip to the nose. Throw again with {guide_mentor}. Compare distances.
Curl the back of the wings up a little. Throw again with {guide_mentor}. Watch how it climbs.
Gather {count} cardboard tubes of different lengths.
Hold one to your ear. Tap the other end. Hear the tone.
Try a different length. Notice how the sound changes.
Line them up shortest to longest with {friend}.
Tap them in order to make a little tune for {friend}.
Pick a quiet corner with {friend}.
Drape one blanket as a roof, another as a door.
Bring in {count} tiny objects that 'belong' to the HQ.
Decide a secret handshake. Practice it three times with {friend}.
Whisper the HQ's {adjective} name to each other.
Pick {count} books from a shelf. Line them up on the floor.
{helper_townsperson} is the librarian. Hand them a pretend card.
Browse the books. Pick one that looks {adjective}.
Bring it to {helper_townsperson}. Get a fake stamp on your card.
Sit nearby and read the first page out loud.
Draw a wiggly path from one side of a paper to the other.
Add {count} dead-end branches that lead nowhere.
Trade papers with {friend}.
Trace {friend}'s maze with one finger from start to end.
Mark the dead ends with an X together.
Find {count} clothespins or rolled paper sticks.
Draw a tiny face on each one with {friend}.
Give each a {adjective} name out loud.
Stand them up in a line. Introduce them to {friend} one at a time.
Pick two characters. Make them say hello to each other in funny voices.
Ask {guide_mentor} for a cup of water with a drop of dish soap.
Stir gently with a spoon.
Dip a paper straw or bent paperclip into the cup.
Lift it out and blow slowly. Watch the bubble grow.
Try a bigger blow. Show {guide_mentor} the biggest bubble you can make.
Cut two paper knights from a folded paper with {friend}.
Color one knight's shield {adjective}. Color the other's a different color.
Stand the knights on a table facing each other.
How do they settle their argument?
Tip the knights toward each other in a slow bow with {friend}. Friends now.
Slide both knights across the table. The first one to fall over loses with a chuckle. {friend} announces the winner.
Ask {guide_mentor} for a spoon of flour on a plate.
Drip three drops of water onto the flour with a straw.
Poke gently with a fingertip. How does it feel?
Mix the spot with a spoon. Make a tiny dough ball.
Roll the dough between your palms. Show {guide_mentor} the smallest ball you can make.
Take a small square of paper. Watch {guide_mentor} fold one corner to the other.
Copy the fold yourself.
Fold the same way one more time.
Pinch one corner up to make a head.
Set the little crane on a windowsill. Tell {guide_mentor} a name for it.
Fold a paper into three equal panels with {friend}.
In the first panel, draw a {adjective} hero.
In the second, draw a problem the hero meets.
In the third, draw how the hero solves it.
Tell the full story to {friend} pointing at each panel in turn.
Gather {count} stuffed animals. Set up each one as a zoo exhibit.
Draw a small {adjective} sign for each animal.
{helper_townsperson} is the visitor. Walk them around.
Tell one fun fact about each animal as you stop at it.
End at the gift shop — a pretend table with one drawing.
Find a balloon or roll a sock into a soft ball.
Stand a few steps from {friend}.
Toss the balloon up high. {friend} tries to catch before it lands.
Switch roles. Count how many catches in a row.
Take one big step back. Try once more with {friend}.
Crumple a paper and smooth it back out. That's the map.
Draw a tiny ship in the corner with {quest_giver}.
Add an X somewhere on the map.
Where does the path go?
Draw a curvy line that loops around a doodled monster. Tell {quest_giver} the monster's name.
Draw a line that crosses three island shapes. Tell {quest_giver} what's hidden on each.
Get a small cup of water and a paintbrush or sponge from {guide_mentor}.
Paint big {adjective} shapes on dark paper with water only.
Watch the marks dry slowly.
Paint the same shape again before the first one is dry.
Tell {guide_mentor} which mark you can still see best.
Fold a small square in half, then again, then again with {guide_mentor}.
Use safety scissors to snip tiny shapes out of the folded edges.
Open it carefully. Look at the {adjective} pattern.
Make a second one with different snips.
Tape both to a window. Wave at {guide_mentor} through the snowflakes.
Spread a blanket on the floor with {friend}.
Bring {count} small snacks or pretend foods on a plate.
Take turns telling each other which is your favorite.
Listen for pretend birds. Cup your ears.
Pack up. Fold the blanket together neatly.
Find a small box and a cardboard tube.
Stand the tube on the box and tape it down. That's the body.
Draw {count} buttons on the chest.
Give the robot a {adjective} name with {friend}.
Press the buttons in order. Each one makes a robot sound — beep, click, hum.
Pick one object in the room. Stare at it for ten breaths.
Turn away. Don't peek.
Draw it from memory with {guide_mentor} watching.
Turn back. Compare your drawing to the real object.
Tell {guide_mentor} one part you got right and one you missed.
Find a pencil. Lay it across the back of a chair so it balances.
Tape a paper cup to each end of the pencil with {guide_mentor}.
Drop a button into one cup. Drop a paperclip into the other.
Watch which side dips. Tell {guide_mentor} which is heavier.
Add more paperclips until the sides match.
Cut {count} paper fish shapes with {friend}.
Slide a paperclip onto each fish's nose.
Tie or tape a magnet to a string on a pencil 'fishing rod'.
Drop the magnet near the fish. Lift one up.
Take turns catching with {friend}. Count how many each.
Cut petal shapes from colored paper with {helper_townsperson}.
Stack {count} petals and pin them with a paperclip in the middle.
Lift each petal slightly to fluff the flower out.
Tape a straw or pencil as a stem.
Stand the flower in a cup. Give it to {helper_townsperson} or a stuffed friend.
Write three sentence starts on a paper with {friend}: 'Once upon a ___ ,' 'They found a ___ ,' 'And they ___ .'
Don't peek at the sentences. {friend} asks: a place, a thing, an action.
Shout out the first {adjective} answer that comes to mind.
Fill the blanks. Read the story aloud.
Laugh together. Try once more with new answers.
Find {count} colored objects around the room.
Lay them in a row from lightest to darkest with {helper_townsperson}.
Now sort by warm or cool — reds in one pile, blues in another.
Pick your favorite color and explain why to {helper_townsperson}.
Put each object back where it came from.
Stack two books a hand's width apart with {guide_mentor}.
Lay a ruler or stick across the gap.
Place a small toy on the bridge. Does it hold?
How to make it stronger?
Slide a second ruler next to the first with {guide_mentor}. Test the toy again.
Fold a paper into a tiny tent under the ruler with {guide_mentor}. Test the toy again.
Draw a five-point star on paper. Use a thick line.
Place your finger at one point.
Breathe in as you trace up to the next point.
Breathe out as you trace down to the point after.
Go all five sides. Show {guide_mentor} how your shoulders feel after.
Ask {guide_mentor} for a small spoon of baking soda in a cup.
Pour a thumb of vinegar from a small pitcher.
Watch the bubbles climb up.
Listen carefully. Tell {guide_mentor} what sound it makes.
Wait for the fizz to stop. Notice the surface settle.
Line {count} pillows up in a path with {friend}.
Place a stuffed animal on each one. They're parade floats.
Carry the floats slowly along the path, one at a time.
Hum a tune as the parade moves with {friend}.
End at the 'parade square'. Take a bow with {friend}.
Punch a tiny hole in two paper cups with {guide_mentor}'s help.
Thread a long string between them and knot each end inside.
Pull the string tight. Each of you holds a cup.
Speak softly into one cup. Listen in the other.
Tell {guide_mentor} what you heard. Try whispering a secret.
Find a small bowl and a wooden spoon.
Add pretend ingredients out loud with {quest_giver}: dragon scale, moonbeam, giggle.
Stir the bowl {count} times clockwise.
Whisper the magic word. Cover the bowl with a cloth.
Lift the cloth slowly. Tell {quest_giver} what the potion does.
Stand a shoebox on its side. That's the dollhouse.
Use small bits of paper as furniture. A bed, a chair, a rug.
Pick a small toy or button to live there.
Walk {friend} through each 'room' as the tour guide.
End by tucking the toy into the bed for a nap.
Pinch a small piece of play dough or kneaded paper with {helper_townsperson}.
Roll it into a ball with your palms.
Pinch two little ears or fins.
Press two tiny dots for eyes.
Set the creature on a paper stage. Make {count} in total.
Write numbers 1 to 6 on small paper squares with {guide_mentor}.
Flip two cards face down on the table.
Pick two cards. Add them. Tell {guide_mentor} the answer.
Pick again. Try one with subtraction.
Stack the cards back up neatly.
Line up {count} stuffed animals with {helper_townsperson}.
Wrap a bandage of paper around one's leg.
Listen to another's heartbeat with a paper-tube stethoscope.
Give each a {adjective} sticker as a treat.
Tell {helper_townsperson} which one needed the most care.
Cut a strip of paper long enough to fit around your head.
Snip zigzag points along one long edge.
Color or decorate the band with {adjective} shapes.
Tape the ends. Place the crown on your head.
Bow to {quest_giver} like royalty.
Fold {count} small paper rectangles into standing pyramid-buildings.
Line them up in two rows like a tiny street.
Draw a road on a larger paper underneath with {friend}.
Add traffic signs as folded paper squares.
Drive a small toy car or button down the road with {friend}.
Pile two pillows in a corner of the couch.
Bring one favorite book to the nest.
Sit with {guide_mentor} nearby.
Read one page quietly. Trace each word with a finger.
Take a square paper. Fold it into a triangle with {guide_mentor}.
Open it. Fold the two side corners to the middle.
Pinch one end to form a tail.
Draw an eye and a friendly smile.
Set the whale on a wave of crumpled blue paper. Show {guide_mentor}.
Line up {count} chairs in a row with {friend}.
That's the train. Sit in the front chair as conductor.
Call out three pretend stations: cookie town, dinosaur valley, sleepy hills.
Make a 'choo-choo' noise between each station.
Pretend to hand out tickets to {friend} at the last stop.
Walk through the room with {helper_townsperson}. Pick up one small interesting thing.
Place it on a piece of paper in the middle.
Draw a {adjective} frame around it like a museum piece.
Write a tiny sign with the object's name.
Show the framed art to {helper_townsperson} and tell its story.
Fold a paper boat with {guide_mentor}.
Float it in a shallow bowl of water.
Drop one button into the boat. Does it still float?
Keep adding buttons one at a time. Count up to {count}.
When it tips, tell {guide_mentor} how many it held.
Pick a stuffed friend to be the dragon with {quest_giver}.
The dragon needs to find {count} shiny things.
Walk around the room with the dragon. Stop at each shiny thing.
Where does the dragon take the loot?

choices.label: To a cave under the pillows
To a tower on top of a chair

steps.text: Tuck the loot under a stack of pillows with {quest_giver}.
Pile the loot on a chair and roar like a dragon with {quest_giver}.
Draw a big {adjective} picture on a thick paper.
Cut it into {count} wavy pieces with safety scissors.
Shuffle the pieces. Mix them up on the table.
Trade with {friend} and put each other's pictures back together.
Cheer when the last piece clicks in.
Draw a plate on paper with {helper_townsperson}.
Add {count} pretend foods around the plate.
Color each one in real food colors.
Read the menu out loud as a chef would.
Serve it to {helper_townsperson} with a big smile.
Cut two paper circles with {guide_mentor}. One big, one small.
Snip teeth around the edges so they look like gears.
Pin them together with a paperclip in the middle so they spin.
Spin one. Watch the other turn the other way.
Tell {guide_mentor} which one moves faster.
Find {count} smooth pebbles or rolled paper balls.
Draw a different {adjective} face on each one.
Give them all names with {friend}.
Stand them in a row like a pebble family.
Take a photo or a quick drawing of them with {friend}.
Look out the window with {guide_mentor}.
Draw the sky on a paper: clouds, sun, anything you see.
Pick up a hairbrush or spoon as a pretend microphone.
Stand in front of your drawing. Announce the weather to {guide_mentor}.
End with a {adjective} forecast for tomorrow.
{quest_giver} hands you a slip with a color name written on it.
Find one thing of that color in the room.
Come back to {quest_giver} for the next color.
Repeat until you've collected {count} colors.
Lay the items in a rainbow with {quest_giver}.
Draw a tiny picture on {count} small paper circles with {friend}.
Flip them face down. Mix them up.
Take turns flipping one and adding a sentence to a story.
Keep going until every circle is used with {friend}.
Tell the whole story start to finish out loud.
Turn a chair on its side with {friend}. That's the ship.
Drape a blanket as a sail.
Place a stuffed friend as the captain.
What's the mission?

choices.label: Trade goods with a friendly port

steps.text: Look under the couch for the cave with {friend}. Bring back one treasure.
Carry pretend goods to a 'port' across the room with {friend}.
Cut two long paper strips with {guide_mentor}.
Tape {count} shorter strips between them as ladder rungs.
Lay the ladder flat. Count the rungs by tapping each one.
Add one more rung. Recount the total.
Show the ladder to {guide_mentor} and call out the new number.
Sit cross-legged near {guide_mentor}.
Take a slow breath in through your nose.
Open your mouth wide and breathe out like a big lion roar.
Now try a tiny mouse breath out.
Crumple {count} small paper balls with {friend}.
Tape them together into one big shape.
Add legs by taping rolled paper sticks underneath.
Decide what creature it is. Tell {friend}.
Set it on a shelf with a tiny paper sign of its name.
Ask {helper_townsperson} for {count} measuring cups of different sizes.
Line them up from smallest to biggest.
Stack them upside-down into a tower.
Knock the tower over gently.
Stack a different way with {helper_townsperson} — biggest on top.
Draw a circle on paper with {guide_mentor}.
Mark four arrows: up is North, down is South, left is West, right is East.
Stand and face the wall in front of you. That's North.
Turn right. Where are you facing now? Tell {guide_mentor}.
Walk three steps east and three steps north.
Find a small notebook with {friend}.
Write down {count} pretend headlines: weather, a missing sock, a new song.
Sit at the table. Use a paper as the news desk.
Announce each headline in a serious voice to {friend}.
Sign off with a {adjective} catchphrase.
Tear a colored paper into small bits with {friend}.
Draw a fish outline on a second paper.
Glue or tape the bits inside the outline like scales.
Add an eye and a tail.
Give the fish a {adjective} name with {friend}.
Get a large paper. Draw the outline of a tiny house with {guide_mentor}.
Mark {count} rooms inside the outline.
Label each room: kitchen, bedroom, secret den.
Add a door and a window with {adjective} curtains.
Walk a small toy through the rooms in order. Show {guide_mentor}.
Cut a wide V-shape from cardboard with {guide_mentor}.
Stand at one end of the hallway.
Toss the boomerang flat across the room.
Does it come back?

choices.label: Throw with a flick of the wrist

steps.text: Bend each tip upward a little. Try again with {guide_mentor}. Watch the path curve.
Add a tiny wrist flick on release. Try again with {guide_mentor}. Notice how it spins.
Lay strips of masking tape on the floor in a winding path with {friend}.
Make {count} dead-end branches off the main path.
Walk through carefully — feet only on the path.
Switch direction. Walk back to the start.
Race {friend} from start to finish in slow motion.
Roll a paper into a long tube. That's the telescope.
Look through it at the ceiling or sky.
Tell {guide_mentor} {count} things you spot — real or pretend.
Move slowly. Whisper each name.
Put the telescope down. Stretch your neck up and look without it.
{quest_giver} hands you a paper map and points to a wavy ocean.
Fold a paper boat together. Place it on the carpet sea.
Wiggle your fingers like rain and rock the boat side to side.
{quest_giver} asks how to handle the wind.

choices.label: Drop the sail and row hard
Raise the sail and surf the gust

steps.text: Hum a low rowing chant and inch the boat across the rug, two hands paddling.
Lift the boat high and zoom across the rug in a long curve.
Land at port. Bow to {quest_giver} and report what you saw out there.
Find a clean cardboard tube, a paper cup, and tape near {friend}.
Tape the cup upside-down on top of the tube for the rocket nose.
Cut three triangles from a paper bag and tape them on as fins.
{friend} asks what color stripe the rocket gets.
Color one sun-yellow stripe down the side of the rocket.
Color one ocean-blue stripe down the side of the rocket.
Color a wiggly rainbow stripe down the side of the rocket.
Hold the rocket high. Count down from five with {friend} and zoom it up to the ceiling.
Find one feather, one tissue, and one small scrap of paper.
Hold them all up to your mouth and blow gently. Watch which floats highest.
Try again with a strong puff. Note which one zooms away first.
Whisper to {friend} which one was your favorite to puff.
{guide_mentor} explains today's three drills: stance, swing, and bow.
Stand tall with feet apart. Hold a rolled-up sock as your sword.
Swing the sock-sword side to side three times, slow and controlled.
{guide_mentor} asks which drill to add.
Raise a pillow as your shield. Block three pretend hits without dropping it.
Charge across {room} with a battle cry, then freeze in a hero pose.
Bow to {guide_mentor} and say 'I am ready, sir or madam.'
{sidekick} hands you a paper notebook and a crayon for sketching.
Walk around {room} pretending you are hunting tiny bugs.
{sidekick} asks what bug you spotted first.
Buzz your lips and hover one hand near {sidekick} like a bumblebee.
Tippy-toe in a tiny line with two fingers like ant feet.
Wave both arms wide and slow like big butterfly wings.
Sketch your bug in the notebook with three crayon strokes.
Show {sidekick} the page and give the bug a silly name.
Set out a bowl, a spoon, and three crumpled paper balls as dough.
Stir the air in the bowl ten times and count out loud.
{friend} asks what shape the cookies should be.
Press each dough ball into a flat star with five points.
Squeeze each dough ball into a wiggly heart shape.
Slide the tray into your pretend oven. Wave your hands like rising heat.
Pull them out, blow gently to cool, and serve one to {friend}.
Find one clean sock. Slip it on your hand like a puppet.
Practice opening and closing the puppet's mouth slowly.
Give the puppet a silly first line — {trickster} suggests something tricky.
What is the puppet's story today?
The puppet searches under a pillow for clues, asking 'have you seen my pair?'
The puppet grumbles 'too tired, too tired' and pretends to yawn.
The puppet hops from foot to foot saying 'so many toes to warm!'
Take a tiny puppet bow and wave the sock at {trickster}.
Lay paper on a sunny patch of floor in {room}.
Place a small object on the paper so it casts a clear shadow.
Carefully trace the shadow's edge with a pencil — go slow.
Whisper to {friend} what shape the shadow looks like now.
{needs_saving} is stuck somewhere. Find six sticky notes or paper scraps.
Stick the notes in a wiggly path across the floor of {room}.
How will you cross the maze?
Hop from note to note without touching the spaces between them.
Crawl on hands and knees, weaving in and out of the notes.
Find {needs_saving} at the end. Lift them up gently and say 'safe at last!'
Pile three pillows into tall towers in one corner of {room}.
Drape a blanket between two towers to make a long wall.
Set {boss_mini_boss} just outside the castle gate, watching.
How do you greet {boss_mini_boss}?

choices.label: Offer a peace flag of folded paper
Stand brave in the doorway with arms wide

steps.text: Fold a paper triangle into a tiny flag. Wave it slowly toward {boss_mini_boss}.
Stand in the gate, plant your feet, and say 'this castle is friendly to all.'
{boss_mini_boss} laughs and sits down for tea. Pour invisible cups all around.

templates.title: Pretend swim with {friend} under the sea

steps.text: Lie on your tummy on the rug. {friend} is your swim buddy.
Wiggle your arms and legs slow, like floating in deep water.
What sea creature do you meet?
Wave eight wiggly arms and spin in a slow circle.
Bob your head up-down-up-down like a tiny sea horse.
Open your arms wide and hum a low whale song.
Swim slowly up. Stand and tell {friend} what you found down below.

templates.title: A message in a bottle from {quest_giver}

steps.text: {quest_giver} hands you a rolled-up paper note with one word peeking out.
Unroll the note carefully. The word is 'treasure'.
Search {room} for one thing that feels like treasure to you.
What did you find?
Hold the shiny treasure up and let it catch the light.
Hug the soft treasure tight against your chest.
Open the book to a favorite page and read one line out loud.
Write a one-word reply on the back of the note and hand it to {quest_giver}.
Stand at one wall of {room} with {sidekick} next to you.
Walk to the other wall like you are made entirely of jelly.
Walk back to the start like a slow-motion robot.
{sidekick} picks the next walk.
Tippy-toe across the room so quiet nobody hears you.
Stomp across the room shaking everything with each step.
Bow to {sidekick}. Say which walk made you giggle the most.
Gather a wooden spoon, an empty cup, and a paper bag.
Tap the spoon on the cup three times for a tiny drum sound.
Crinkle the paper bag for a shaker rhythm.
What song do you and {friend} play?
Tap-tap-tap softly, one beat every two seconds, like a lullaby.
Tap and crinkle quickly together — march in place with the rhythm.
Take a band bow with {friend}. Wave at your invisible audience.
Gather {count} small pebbles, beads, or buttons.
Line them up in a row on the floor of {room}.
Touch each one and count out loud, very softly.
{guide_mentor} hands you dark paper and a white crayon.
Draw five small dots across the page — those are stars.
Draw one big swirl in the middle — that is your galaxy.
{guide_mentor} asks what planet to add first.

choices.label: A red-rock world with a moon

steps.text: Draw a blue circle with two thin rings around it.
Draw a red circle with one small white moon nearby.
Draw a fuzzy gray circle with question marks floating around it.
Whisper a name for your galaxy. Tell {guide_mentor} the name proudly.
Line up four toys, biggest first, behind {friend}.
March them slowly across {room} in a wobbly straight line.
Stop the parade. Have each toy take a tiny bow in turn.
What parade music plays now?
Hum a loud trumpet tune and high-step the toys in place.
Hum a soft flute tune and sway the toys gently.
Clap three times. {friend} bows for the whole parade.
Pile two pillows into a tall lumpy volcano in one corner.
Lay a blanket flat as an island around the volcano.
Place {boss_mini_boss} on top of the volcano like a guardian.
How do you approach the volcano?

choices.label: Sing a friendly hello song from the front

steps.text: Sneak around in slow motion. Touch the volcano with one careful finger.
Sing a happy hello tune and wave at {boss_mini_boss}.
The volcano puffs! Wiggle your hands like sparkles falling all around.
Walk to the nearest window in {room}.
Look up and find one cloud that has a clear shape.
Tell {friend} what shape it looks like to you.
Watch it slowly drift for one whole minute. Notice what changes.
{trickster} hands you a folded paper with three squiggly symbols.
The first symbol means 'jump.' Jump up three times.
The second symbol means 'spin.' Spin in a slow circle.
{trickster} asks what the third symbol means.
Whisper 'banana-boots' into your cupped hands.
Take a deep bow toward {trickster}.
Wave both hands at the ceiling like you are saying hi to the stars.
{trickster} grins. Say 'code cracked!' and high-five the air.
Find a cup and a spoon. {guide_mentor} is your potion teacher.
Mix three pretend ingredients: a leaf, a button, a whisper.
Stir the cup slowly while you count to ten.
{guide_mentor} asks what the potion does.

choices.label: Makes you giggle for a moment
Makes you brave for a moment
Makes you fast for a moment

steps.text: Sip the air-potion and burst into a tiny laugh.
Sip the air-potion and stand tall with hands on hips.
Sip the air-potion and zoom around once in fast circles.
Bow to {guide_mentor} and report how the potion felt.
Set out a folded towel as a hospital bed in the corner of {room}.
Lay {needs_saving} gently on the towel. Pat their head softly.
What is {needs_saving}'s problem today?
Wipe their nose with a pretend tissue and whisper kind words.
Wrap a strip of paper around the paw like a bandage.
Hold their hand and tell them three things that make you feel safe.
Help {needs_saving} stand up and dance one slow step together.
Lay a long string or belt straight across the floor.
Step onto one end with arms out wide.
Walk to the other end without falling off the line.
How do you walk back?
Heel-to-toe, count each step out loud.
On tippy-toes, hold your breath and balance.
Walk backwards, careful not to peek over the wrong shoulder.
Take a circus bow for {friend} who watched the whole show.
Find {count} socks in a pile from the laundry basket.
With {helper_townsperson}, lay them all out on the floor.
Match each sock to its partner by color or pattern.
Roll each matched pair into a tiny bundle.
{frenemy} is sitting in the middle of {room} with arms crossed.
Walk up and bow politely. Say 'hello, friend-enemy.'
What deal do you offer?

choices.label: Trade three giggles for a high-five
Take turns being leader for one minute

steps.text: Giggle three times. {frenemy} gives you a slow high-five.
March in a line. Switch who is in front every ten seconds.
Shake hands with {frenemy}. The deal is sealed.
Imagine {room} is a vast hot desert. {sidekick} walks at your side.
Take {count} slow heavy steps across the sand.
Stop. Cup your hands around your eyes and scan for an oasis.
What do you find in the sand?
Dig with both hands and pull out the imaginary coin. Show it to {sidekick}.
Crouch and trace the giant footprint with one finger.
Unfold the imaginary map and point to the next stop.
Reach the oasis. Splash pretend water on your face with {sidekick}.
Set out two cups and two plates on a small flat surface.
Pour pretend tea into both cups with a careful spout-tilt.
Place an invisible cookie on each plate.
What do you and {friend} talk about?
Discuss moon weather in serious tones. Sip slowly.
Argue politely about the best sock. Sip slowly.
Whisper a theory about dust bunny villages. Sip slowly.
Clear the cups together. Thank {friend} for the lovely chat.
Sit on the floor next to {sidekick} with crossed legs.
Slowly raise both arms above your head. Hold for three breaths.
Bend forward gently to touch your toes — or your knees.
{sidekick} picks the next pose.
On hands and knees, arch your back high like a stretching cat.
Curl into a ball with your forehead on the floor like a turtle.
Sit tall again. Take three slow breaths with {sidekick}.
Lay out three toys in a row. They are clockwork citizens.
'Wind up' the first toy with a twisty finger motion behind its back.
Move it forward three small steps like ticking clockwork.
Which citizen winds up next?
Wind the tall toy and march it three big slow steps.
Wind the small toy and skip it three tiny quick steps.
Wind the fluffy toy and roll it three soft tumbles.
Line them all up at the finish. Bow to {friend} the mayor.
Sit very still in a comfy spot in {room}.
Close your eyes and breathe in slow for four counts.
Listen for three different sounds. Don't talk yet.
Open your eyes. Whisper the three sounds to {friend}.
Fold two small paper boats together with {friend}.
Set them at one end of a long table or smooth floor.
Blow gently on your boat to push it forward.
What kind of wind today?
Blow slow steady breaths. Move your boat one boat-length at a time.
Blow strong puffs in bursts. Race in stops and starts.
Whichever boat crosses first, cheer for the other one too.
{guide_mentor} pulls a book off the shelf with a serious look.
Open the book to any page and pretend to read the magic words.
Trace one word with your finger and say it out loud.
What spell are you learning?
Stretch as tall as you can with arms reaching up. Hold for three.
Wiggle your fingers and pretend to glow brighter and brighter.
Freeze perfectly still. Even your breath is silent.
Close the book softly. Bow to {guide_mentor}.

templates.title: Be a {adjective} robot for a minute

steps.text: Stand tall like a robot. {friend} is your operator.
{friend} says 'walk forward' — take five robot steps.
What command comes next?
Beep-boop a single tune in three notes.
Jerk your arms up and down stiffly to a beat.
Snap one hand to your forehead in a sharp salute.
Power down slowly. Crouch and close your eyes.
Stack three pillows tall to be the tower in one corner.
Place {needs_saving} on top of the tower carefully.
Plan your rescue route across {room}.
{helper_townsperson} hands you a rescue tool.

choices.label: A bouncy crash pad of blankets

steps.text: Twist scarves into a rope. Toss one end up and pretend to pull {needs_saving} down.
Pile blankets at the tower's base. Lift {needs_saving} and lower them gently to the pad.
Wrap {needs_saving} in a hug. Say 'you're safe now.'
Lay three crayons in front of you: red, blue, yellow.
Point to the color that feels happy today.
Point to the color that feels calm.
Tell {friend} which feeling is biggest in you right now.
Imagine you and {sidekick} are inside a moon rover.
Buckle pretend seat belts with a click-click.
Steer with a giant invisible wheel as you drive across {room}.
What do you spot on the moon?
Park the rover. Peek into the crater. Wonder out loud how deep it goes.
Hop out and bounce a few low-gravity jumps to chase the rock.
Wave back at the alien with both hands.
Climb back in. Drive home and report your findings to {sidekick}.
Pretend the biggest bowl in {room} is a stew pot.
{trickster} drops in a 'sock noodle' with a wink.
Add a sprinkle of imaginary giggle-pepper.
How do we stir?

choices.label: Stir by spinning the pot in circles

steps.text: Bend your elbows and pretend to stir with them.
Tilt your head as you stir in slow loops.
Spin the imaginary pot in circles, careful not to spill.
Pretend to taste a tiny spoonful. Make your silliest yum face.
Sit in a quiet spot with paper and one crayon.
Draw one slow line all the way across the page.
Add three small dots wherever feels right.
Show {friend} the doodle. Don't explain it — just smile.
{quest_giver} says a dragon egg is hidden somewhere in {room}.
Crouch low and crawl in a slow careful sweep.
Sniff the air for dragon-egg smell — a little smoky, a little sweet.
Where do you check first?
Lift the pillow with care and peek beneath.
Peek behind the curtain or door with a soft tiptoe.
Reach into the basket or bowl very gently.
You found a small round 'egg' object! Cradle it back to {quest_giver}.
Sit in a circle with {sidekick} and {friend} on the floor.
Take turns making one animal sound, quietly to start.
Layer the sounds together: moo, meow, hoot.
Who leads the next tempo?
Match the fast jungle beat with chirps and squeaks.
Match the slow ocean hum with soft whale calls.
End the symphony all at once with a giant 'shhhh'.
Grab a soft ball or rolled-up sock. {friend} is your co-inventor.
Make up one rule: you have to hop while holding the ball.
Add a second rule.

choices.label: Shout a fruit name on each pass

steps.text: Try passing with your elbow three times.
Shout a fruit name each time you pass. Banana! Apple!
Spin once before each pass. Make yourself a little dizzy.
Give the new sport a silly name. Announce it to {friend}.
Sit in a small circle with two toys and {friend}.
One toy starts the story: 'Once upon a quiet evening...'
Pass the story to the next toy with a sentence.
End with {friend}'s sentence: '... and that's how it ended.'
{big_bad_boss} sits at one end of {room} — a giant pretend rhino.
Stand at the other end with {sidekick}. Plan an escape.
How do you outrun the rhino?

choices.label: Hide behind a pillow fort halfway

steps.text: Zig-zag in tight angles, never running in a straight line.
Sprint, then dive behind the pillow fort and hold still.
Reach the safe zone! High-five {sidekick} and breathe big breaths.
Lay a big sheet of paper down. {friend} is your art partner.
Dip a finger into pretend paint and swirl one big cloud.
Use another finger for a glowing pretend sun.
What else lives in your sky?
Dot three small birds across the page.
Sweep a big curved rainbow across the page.
Add a soft moon peeking out from the corner.
Show {friend} the finished sky and bow.
{trickster} hands you an invisible spy badge. Clip it on proudly.
Tip-toe across {room} with hands behind your back like a real spy.
Hide behind a couch or chair. Peek out with one eye.
What do you spy?
Crawl toward the treasure pillow and tag it gently.
Follow the shadow with two careful steps, then freeze.
Unfold the imaginary note, read it, and tuck it away.
Return to {trickster} and whisper your mission report.
Spread a small towel as your market stall in {room}.
Place {count} small toys or objects on the towel as wares.
{helper_townsperson} is your first customer. Greet them politely.
What does the customer want to trade?
Accept the buttons. Hand the customer one ware with a bow.
Hum a quick tune in trade. Hand over one ware.
Tell a tiny joke. Hand over one ware to {helper_townsperson}.
Wave goodbye and fold up your market towel.
Stack three blocks or cups in a tower in front of {friend}.
Each block is one part of a story: beginning, middle, end.
Touch the top block. Say one sentence: 'Once there was...'
Touch the middle block. Say 'Then...' and finish the sentence.
Touch the bottom block. Say 'And finally...' and close the story.
{guide_mentor} explains today is a friendly tournament. No real swords.
Bow to {friend}, your opponent. Take three paces apart.
Hold a rolled-sock sword high. Shield arm out wide.
Pick your round.
Swing the slowest sword arc you can. Move like underwater.
Yell the silliest battle cry — 'Banana-yo!' or your own.
Faint dramatically to the floor. Twitch one foot.
{guide_mentor} declares a tie. Everyone bows and laughs.
Line up four pillows in a row. They are train cars.
Sit on the first pillow. {friend} sits on the second.
Shout 'all aboard!' and pretend to pull a whistle cord.
What is the next stop?
Pull into snack mountain. Hand {friend} a pretend muffin.
Pull into sleepy valley. Yawn together for a long pretend rest.
Pull into giggle gulch. Laugh as loud as you can for five seconds.
Pull the whistle one last time. Wave goodbye from the train.

templates.title: Build a fairy moss garden with {guide_mentor}

steps.text: Find a tray or shallow dish. {guide_mentor} watches helpfully.
Place soft scraps of paper or fabric inside as 'moss'.
Add three tiny pebbles or beads as fairy stepping stones.
What lives in the garden?

choices.label: A song-fairy who hums all day

steps.text: Cup your hands around the garden. Pretend the fairy is glowing.
Hum a tiny tune over the garden — the fairy's song.
Bow to {guide_mentor} and leave the garden in a quiet spot.
Pile three pillows into a curved submarine shape.
Crawl inside with {sidekick}. Pull the lid pillow over.
Peer through a 'periscope' — your hands cupped around your eyes.
What do you see down deep?

choices.label: A sunken ship with a treasure

steps.text: Wiggle fingers in front of your eyes like darting fish.
Pretend to scoop up the treasure with two careful hands.
Wave one arm slowly like a giant octopus saying hello.
Surface the submarine. Climb out and report to {sidekick}.
{helper_townsperson} hands you a pretend basket.
Walk slowly to a corner of {room}. There are pretend eggs to gather.
Bend and pick three imaginary eggs, very carefully.
What chore is next?
Scatter pretend seeds and cluck like a chicken alongside them.
Brush the imaginary cow's back with long slow strokes.
Wave goodbye to the animals and hand the basket back to {helper_townsperson}.
Lay out four crayons of different colors.
Look at each color and breathe one slow breath.
Move the most calming color closer to you.
Tell {friend} why that color feels calmest right now.
Fold one paper plane together with {friend}.
Decide today's pretend weather is windy.
Hold the plane high. Let it glide across {room}.
Now switch the weather.
Flutter your fingers like snowflakes as the plane wobbles down.
Glide the plane smooth and long, soaring through the sunshine.
Pretend rain pours — bring the plane down quickly and gently.
Tell {friend} which weather the plane liked best.
{trickster} whispers that {room} has three friendly ghosts today.
Tip-toe to the first 'ghost' — point at a corner and wave.
Pretend to listen as the ghost giggles back.
What does ghost number two say?
Boop the air gently with your fingertip. Smile back.
Sweep your arm in a whoosh motion across your body.
Wave goodbye to all three ghosts. {trickster} nods, pleased.
Gather three stuffed friends and line them up.
Tuck each one in with a soft blanket fold.
Whisper goodnight to each, with their own quiet wave.
Stand in front of a mirror with {friend}.
Look at your reflection and smile a big bright smile.
What compliment does the mirror give you?
Say 'I AM a great friend' back to the mirror.
Say 'I DO make people laugh' back to the mirror.
Say 'I AM brave and kind' back to the mirror.
Bow to your reflection. Thank the mirror with {friend}.

templates.title: Suit up to meet {big_bad_boss} the asteroid

steps.text: Stand tall. Pull on an invisible space helmet over your head.
Click pretend boots onto your feet. Stomp once to test.
Tighten an imaginary belt full of tools.
What does {big_bad_boss} the asteroid need from you?
Sweep big slow arm motions to brush off pretend dust.
Pretend to plant a flag, then stand back and salute it.
Lean close and whisper 'hi, big rock, you are welcome here.'
Float slowly back to your spaceship and wave at {big_bad_boss}.
Sit cross-legged with {friend} facing each other.
Snap or clap a slow steady beat — one, two, one, two.
{friend} adds a twist.

choices.label: Add a knee tap on beat two
Add a head nod on beat one

steps.text: Snap, knee-tap, snap, knee-tap — keep it tight.
Nod-snap, snap, nod-snap, snap — match {friend}.
Stop on the same beat together. High-five {friend}.

templates.title: Crown {needs_saving} as friend of the day

steps.text: Cut or fold a strip of paper into a tiny crown.
Color or decorate the crown with one bright marker stroke.
{needs_saving} sits in a chair very seriously.
What title goes with the crown?
Place the crown gently. Announce 'Royal Snack Finder!'
Place the crown gently. Announce 'Royal Story Inventor!'
Place the crown gently. Announce 'Royal High-Five Champion!'
Bow deeply to {needs_saving}. Lead a tiny round of applause.
Stand on a 'deck' — a couch cushion or a flat patch of {room}.
Hum a low pirate tune in three notes. {sidekick} joins in.
Add an arr-arr on every other line.
What is the shanty about?

choices.label: The treasure we never quite reach

steps.text: Sing 'Oh how I miss my peanut butter,' with arr-arrs.
Sing 'That bird, that bird, she's too clever,' with arr-arrs.
Sing 'The chest is buried just past Tuesday,' with arr-arrs.
Drop anchor with a thump. Bow to {sidekick}.
Line three pillows in a row with a tiny gap between each.
Drape a blanket over the top to make a tunnel.
Get on your hands and knees with {friend} behind you.
How do you go through?
Crawl one careful inch at a time. Don't bump the roof.
Wiggle like a worm with your arms tucked in.
Pop out the other side. Cheer loud for {friend}.
Stand or sit with feet flat. Breathe in slow.
Reach both hands up toward the ceiling, slow and steady.
Lower hands to your sides. Roll shoulders back twice.
Smile at {friend} and say 'I feel a little softer now.'
{quest_giver} hands you a paper compass with N, E, S, W marked.
Point at the front wall of {room} — that's pretend north.
Turn 90 degrees right. That's east.
{quest_giver} sends you in a direction.
Turn west and take three slow steps.
Turn south and take four slow steps.
Turn between north and east. Skip twice that way.
Wave at {quest_giver} and report your new spot.
{trickster} announces today everything is backwards.
Walk three steps backwards. Look over your shoulder for safety.
Say 'goodbye' instead of 'hello' to {trickster}. Wave anyway.
What's the next backwards thing?

choices.label: Sing a song from the last line first

steps.text: Pretend to spoon up invisible cereal at the wrong time of day.
Hum a song starting from the last line you remember.
Wave 'hello' (instead of goodbye) and end the backwards game.
Sit close to {friend}. Whisper a single short word.
{friend} whispers the word back exactly the same way.
Whisper a second word, even softer this time.
Each of you holds a rolled paper or cardboard tube — a pretend lance.
Stand ten steps apart with {friend} across the room.
Trot toward each other in slow motion, lances forward.
What kind of friendly hit?

choices.label: Both shout 'huzzah!' and miss on purpose

steps.text: Tap the lances tip to tip. Take a courteous bow.
Both shout 'huzzah!' and zoom past each other, laughing.
Turn and bow to {friend}. The joust is friendly and done.
Tie a long piece of string or ribbon to a paper square.
Hold the string. {sidekick} stands by to admire.
Run a few steps so the kite trails behind.
What does the kite do in the air?
Jerk the string up and down so the kite makes loops.
Hold the string steady and high. The kite hovers in the air.
Wiggle the string side to side for a snaky dance.
Reel the kite in slowly. Show {sidekick} how it landed.
Imagine the floor is a pumpkin patch. {helper_townsperson} guides you.
Bend low and pretend to pat a round pumpkin.
Which pumpkin is yours?

choices.label: The biggest one in the patch
The wobbliest one in the patch

steps.text: Hug the giant pumpkin with both arms. Pretend to carry it.
Wobble with the wobbly pumpkin in your arms. Don't drop it.
Cradle the tiny pumpkin in your hands like a chick.
Wave goodbye to {helper_townsperson} and carry your pumpkin home.
Sit in a small circle with {sidekick}.
Whisper a short message into {sidekick}'s ear.
{sidekick} pretends to pass it on to a stuffed toy.
Say the message out loud at the end. See if it survived.
{guide_mentor} dims the lights and points at the ceiling.
Lie on your back on a blanket. Look up at the pretend stars.
Wait quietly. Watch for a streak of pretend light.
You see one! What do you wish?

choices.label: A kind wish for someone you love

steps.text: Whisper a kind wish into the air above you.
Whisper a silly wish — maybe pancakes that roar?
Lie still a little longer. Thank {guide_mentor} with a sleepy wave.
Find a paper plate or a flat piece of cardboard.
Draw two eyes and one mouth on the plate.
Tape a craft stick or pencil to the back as a handle.
What feeling does the plate face show?
